alerts.title: Added: Singapore Additive Limits - Class II chemical preservatives
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Within prescribed regulatory limits. Class II chemical preservatives can only be directly added to fillings and toppings in kuehs, within regulatory limits.

alerts.title: Added: Singapore Additive Limits - Class II chemical preservatives (e.g., sorbic acid, benzoic acid)
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Not to be directly added. Class II chemical preservatives should not be directly added during the manufacturing of kueh.

alerts.title: Added: Singapore Additive Limits - Food additives
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Check official source. Food additives must undergo safety assessment by SFA before use in food sold in Singapore.

alerts.title: Added: Singapore Additive Limits - Unpermitted food additives
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Prohibited. Import or sale of food products containing unpermitted additives is prohibited.

alerts.title: Added: Singapore Contaminant Limits - Contaminants
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Check official source. All food businesses must comply with regulatory limits on contaminants.

alerts.title: Added: Singapore Contaminant Limits - Contaminants (incidental constituents)
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Within SFA’s regulatory limits. Contaminants in food sold in Singapore must comply with SFA's regulatory limits.

alerts.title: Added: Singapore Contaminant Limits - Contaminants exceeding limits
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Prohibited. Import or sale of food products containing contaminants exceeding regulatory limits is prohibited.

alerts.title: Added: Singapore Contaminant Limits - Veterinary drug residues (diethylstibestrol, hexoestrol, dienoestrol)
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Prohibited. Import or sale of meat and food derived from meat containing these specific veterinary drug residues is prohibited under Regulation 33 of the Food Regulations.

alerts.title: Added: Singapore Labelling Requirement - Ingredients and additives
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Must be declared. All ingredients and additives used in prepacked food products must be declared in the list of ingredients on food labels.

alerts.title: Added: Codex Additive Limits - Permitted food additives
alerts.details: New: Verification required via GSFA Online Database (Codex STAN 192-1995). The Codex General Standard for Food Additives (GSFA) sets forth the conditions for permitted food additives in all foods. Users are encouraged to consult the database and its Preamble for specific provisions.

alerts.title: Removed: Codex Additive Limits - Specific food additives
alerts.details: Old: Verification required via GSFA database. Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

alerts.title: Added: Codex Contaminant Limits - Contaminants and naturally occurring toxicants
alerts.details: New: Verification required via General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995). Codex establishes maximum levels (MLs) and guideline levels for contaminants and naturally occurring toxicants in food and feed. These levels are legally permitted concentrations.

alerts.title: Modified: Codex Contaminant Limits - Mycotoxins
alerts.details: Old: Verification required via CXC 78-2017 Code of Practice for the Prevention and Reduction of Mycotoxins in Spices. New: Follow Code of Practice for the Prevention and Reduction of Mycotoxins in Spices. Specific code of practice for the prevention and reduction of mycotoxin contamination in spices.

alerts.title: Removed: Codex Contaminant Limits - Contaminants and naturally occurring toxicants
alerts.details: Old: Verification required via Codex General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995) and CCCF established levels. Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

alerts.title: Added: European Union Additive Limits - Food additives
alerts.details: New: Verification required. The EU Additives Database, based on Annex II of Regulation (EC) No 1333/2008, provides information on approved food additives and their conditions of use in the EU.

alerts.title: Removed: European Union Labelling Requirement - Source fetch / extraction issue
alerts.details: Old: Manual review required. Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

alerts.title: Added: European Union Microbiology Limits - Foodborne bacteria, toxins, and metabolites (e.g., Salmonella, Listeria monocytogenes, Enterobacter sakazakii, staphylococcal enterotoxins, histamine)
alerts.details: New: Verification required. Commission Regulation (EC) No 2073/2005 lays down food safety criteria for relevant foodborne bacteria, their toxins and metabolites in specific foods, and process hygiene criteria.

alerts.title: Removed: Japan Additive Limits - Additives (excluding natural flavoring agents and generally consumed substances used as additives)
alerts.details: Old: Prohibited unless specified as unlikely to cause harm by the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare. Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

alerts.title: Removed: Japan Additive Limits - Methods of producing, processing, using, cooking, or preserving food or additives; Ingredients of food or additives
alerts.details: Old: Must conform to criteria/standards established by the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare. Verification required.. Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

alerts.title: Removed: Japan Contaminant Limits - Contamination or foreign substances
alerts.details: Old: Prohibited if likely to cause harm due to contamination or addition of foreign substances. Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

alerts.title: Removed: Japan Contaminant Limits - Residues of agricultural chemicals, feed additives, or animal pharmaceuticals
alerts.details: Old: Must not exceed quantities specified by the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare as unlikely to cause harm. Verification required.. Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

alerts.title: Removed: Japan Contaminant Limits - Toxic or harmful substances
alerts.details: Old: Prohibited, unless specified as harmless by MHLW. Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

alerts.title: Removed: Japan Labelling Requirement - Food business operator responsibility for safety and information
alerts.details: Old: Must endeavor to ensure safety, obtain knowledge, ensure raw material safety, conduct voluntary inspections, record necessary information (e.g., seller name), and take prompt measures for hazards.. Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

alerts.title: Removed: Japan Microbiology Limits - Handling of food or additives for sale
alerts.details: Old: Must be collected, produced, processed, used, cooked, stored, transported, displayed, and delivered in a clean and sanitary manner.. Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

alerts.title: Removed: Japan Microbiology Limits - Pathogens
alerts.details: Old: Prohibited if contaminated with pathogens and likely to cause harm. Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

alerts.title: Added: Singapore Additive Limits - Food additive limits
alerts.details: New: Verification required. Specific limits for food additives vary by additive and food product type. Refer to SFA's Guidance Information.

alerts.title: Added: Singapore Additive Limits - Multiple Class II chemical preservatives
alerts.details: New: Calculation tool required. When using two or more Class II chemical preservatives, the SFA's Calculation Tool must be used to determine permitted levels.

alerts.title: Added: Singapore Additive Limits - Permitted food additives
alerts.details: New: Verification required. Businesses must use the SFA's List of Permitted Food Additives or Food Additives Search tool to verify if additives are allowed.

alerts.title: Removed: Singapore Additive Limits - Class II chemical preservatives (e.g., sorbic acid, benzoic acid)
alerts.details: Old: Calculation tool must be used for permitted levels if multiple are present. Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.
